topic: NH heat excess asymmetry — defense neutralization verification (EXP-296)
change_summary: EXP-296 P1 landed: the trailing single-sentence sign-non-discrimination clause in detail_verdict_text was replaced with a labeled "Defender pivot anticipated" paragraph containing three numbered counters. Patch text matches the EXP-296 spec verbatim (find string of length 220 chars replaced once, expansion to ~1,800 chars). Detail_evidence inverse-cube wording fix from c4 hole #5 is also now present (must have landed via a separate decider self-apply, since it predates this commit and is unchanged in the EXP-296 diff). Verifying: (a) does the new paragraph actually close ISS-1917 as filed; (b) does it introduce any new factual or rhetorical problems; (c) what hole landscape remains for the next cycle.
our_argument_summary: Verdict 'Std Model Explains' continues to hold. Hemispheric energy balance is near-symmetric (Stephens et al. 2012, doi:10.1038/ngeo1580); the +0.34 W/m^2/decade NH-SH absorbed-solar-radiation trend (Loeb et al. 2025 PNAS, doi:10.1073/pnas.2511595122) is an aerosol/albedo/cloud-driven 24-year trend, not a static asymmetry. The dome supplies no radiation-budget derivation; under the inverse-cube projected-flux integral that follows from h=5,733 km geometry, the predicted NH-SH difference is ~100-300 W/m^2 across the dome's realistic seasonal sun-orbit range — wrong by 250-800×. EXP-296 now adds a labeled defender-pivot-anticipation paragraph to detail_verdict_text that traps a dome advocate retreating to 'we only predicted sign, not magnitude' between three counters: (1) the sign is independently predicted by mainstream science via land-mass and aerosol mechanisms (Loeb 2025 abstract quoted verbatim), (2) the dome cites a specific value as confirmation, not just any positive difference, and (3) disclaiming the inverse-cube integral leaves the dome with no derivation at all, making the +0.34 match content-free.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (V50.6 baseline; WIN-066 not present — V51 addition)
raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt (V51 capture is WIN-064-only — WIN-066 prose not in this snapshot)
monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-066.json (analyst confirms no monitor.py / repo code references WIN-066, hemispheric energy, CERES, or 0.34)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c2/c3/c4: no dome-side calculation pipeline for WIN-066. monitor.py has no domain entry. No CERES pull, no hemispheric integral, no W/m^2 comparison logic. The dome publishes the WIN as a confirmed prediction in V51.0 by adopting the published CERES-derived figure.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Not applicable — no validation logic of any kind exists for this WIN.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None observed in dome code. The patch (EXP-296 P1) is entirely on our side; no dome-side code change.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. monitoring=none, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false, relabels_standard=true all remain correctly tagged.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Confirmed against current globe-fingerprint code_search.found_in: 'No direct reference to WIN-066 or hemispheric energy in dome raw text. WIN-066 is a V51.0 addition not present in the V50.6 baseline snapshot.' c2/c3/c4 also confirmed against fuller raw-text snapshots. EXP-296 P1 changed only our prose; dome monitor.py is unchanged.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Stephens et al. 2012 (Nature Geoscience) baselined hemispheric symmetry. Loeb et al. 2025 (PNAS) attributes the small NH-SH trend specifically to 'changes in hemispheric differences in aerosol-radiation interactions, surface albedo, and water vapor changes' — i.e., entirely standard-physics drivers (anthropogenic aerosols, ice-albedo feedback, water vapor). The dome contributes no new numerical prediction; it adopts +0.34 W/m^2/decade and reframes geometric asymmetry as the cause.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Loeb et al. 2025 was published online 2025-09-29 (print 2025-10-07, PNAS issue 40), well before V51.0 (2026). CERES TOA flux datasets have been publicly indexed since ~2000. The dome's adoption of +0.34 post-dates the publication; no monitor.py entry exists for the value, so the weaker commit-timestamp test is inapplicable. Detail_verdict_text correctly invokes Part 3.1b's 'not plausibly known to the author at write-time' carve-out and shows it does not apply.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No dome formula starts from disc_radius, firmament_height, sun_altitude, or any aether parameter and arrives at +0.34 W/m^2/decade. Counter (3) of the new defender-pivot paragraph captures this with the dilemma framing: either the dome owns the inverse-cube integral (and is wrong by 250-800×) or it has no quantitative prediction at all.

holes_found.description: Counter (1) of the new defender-pivot paragraph appeals to globe-physics mechanisms (~39% NH land vs ~19% SH land, albedo asymmetry, aerosol-radiation interactions per Loeb 2025) to argue that the NH > SH sign is non-discriminating. A sophisticated dome defender could call this circular: 'You are using globe-physics albedo arguments to refute a dome model. Of course standard climate science predicts NH > SH if you accept the underlying globe physics — but I am precisely contesting that physics applies in the dome geometry.' The Counter (1) framing as written presents the land-mass and aerosol mechanisms as if they are model-neutral observational facts. They are partly observational (continent areas are measurable independently of the globe/dome dispute) and partly mechanistic (albedo-driven absorption presumes standard radiative-transfer physics that a dome defender could in principle dispute). Counter (3) does the heavy lifting of pinning the defender to the inverse-cube integral or no prediction at all, but Counter (1) on its own would be vulnerable to a 'you are assuming what you should prove' rebuttal.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional small tightening: change 'differential land-mass distribution (~39% NH vs ~19% SH)' to 'directly observable land-mass distribution (~39% NH vs ~19% SH continental coverage)' to make the mechanism's empirical anchor explicit; or add a half-sentence noting that the land-mass percentages are direct continental measurements independent of the globe/dome geometry dispute. Severity is minor because Counter (3) carries the squeeze regardless of how Counter (1) is read; the rebuttal is structurally robust even if a single counter could be picked apart in isolation.

holes_found.description: Counter (2) asserts 'The dome cites a specific value (+0.34 W/m^2/decade) as confirmation, not "any positive NH-SH difference."' This depends on the dome's V51 page actually citing the +0.34 figure verbatim as confirmation. The current globe-fingerprint code_search confirms 'No direct reference to WIN-066 or hemispheric energy in dome raw text' — the V51 WIN-066 prose is not in the raw-text snapshot we have on file (raw-text/02-wins.txt is V50.6 baseline, raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt is WIN-064-only). The dome page presumably does cite +0.34 (this WIN's whole structure rests on matching that published value), but our local source-of-truth doesn't directly verify it. If a dome defender contested 'we never committed to +0.34 specifically; we only said geometric asymmetry produces an NH excess of unspecified magnitude,' Counter (2) would be vulnerable. Severity minor because (a) detail_claim asserts the dome 'predicts persistent Northern Hemisphere energy absorption excess of +0.34 W/m^2', (b) prior reviewers (analyst globe-fingerprint reviewed_at 2026-04-09 documented dome adopting 'CERES-derived +0.34 W/m^2/decade'), and (c) the pull_data.py / monitor.py grep already showed no dome code-side pre-registration so there is nothing to refute.
holes_found.recommendation: Refresh the raw-text snapshot for WIN-066 the next time the poller scrapes the dome's V51 predictions page and store the dome's verbatim wording in raw-text/02-wins-V51-win066.txt. If the dome page does cite +0.34 explicitly, link to that fingerprint from detail_verdict_text. If it does not (the dome may have phrased the claim more loosely), Counter (2) needs a one-word softening to 'cites the published +0.34 W/m^2/decade as the confirming value' rather than committing the dome to that specific number. Optional, not blocking. Could be folded into a future analyst Mode 2b re-examination of WIN-066.

holes_found.description: ISS-1908 PERSISTS — RANGE-WIDTH UNDERIVED. EXP-296 P1 was scoped to defender-pivot rebuttal only and explicitly did not touch the '90-270 W/m^2' range or its derivation in detail_evidence. My c4 re-integration computed ~100-300 W/m^2 across realistic seasonal sun-orbit radii (rs ≈ 7,400 km Tropic of Cancer to rs ≈ 12,600 km Tropic of Capricorn), so the lower-bound 90 in detail_evidence is still barely defensible (matches only the very high-rs end of the seasonal range under a slightly different normalization). The new defender-pivot paragraph in detail_verdict_text repeats the '90-270 W/m^2' range and the '~250-800×' over-prediction factor, propagating the c2-inherited framing without tightening. Not introduced by EXP-296; carried over from prior cycles.
holes_found.recommendation: Folded into the existing ISS-1908 issue — no new issue needed. Recommend: a future analyst patch (already flagged by EXP-296.holes_not_addressed scope_note) tightens detail_evidence to '~100-300 W/m^2 across the dome's realistic seasonal sun-orbit radius (Tropic of Cancer ~7,400 km in June through Tropic of Capricorn ~12,600 km in December), with annual mean of order 180-200 W/m^2' and synchronously updates the defender-pivot paragraph's '90-270 W/m^2' citation to match. The '~250-800×' over-prediction factor is correct for the tightened range and need not change.

holes_found.description: EXP-296 itself flags but explicitly does not patch a cross-WIN systemic issue: sign-match-non-discrimination is a recurring failure mode across WIN-058/-059/-060 (NMP/SAA drift directional matches) and other directional-only confirmations. The defender-pivot paragraph closes the loophole for WIN-066 specifically but does not establish a Section 4 (Axial Symmetry Paradox) or cross-WIN audit trail naming this pattern. As long as the same pattern recurs WIN-by-WIN, motivated dome defenders can attempt the sign-only retreat on each new WIN and force per-WIN rebuttals. Severity minor because each per-WIN closure is independently sound; the cost is rhetorical-economy not correctness.
holes_found.recommendation: Out of scope for this c5 verification. Surface as either (a) a holistic-review item in the curmudgeon tracker (cross-WIN sign-match-non-discrimination sweep) or (b) an analyst Section 4 expansion adding a named subsection 'Sign-only predictions are non-discriminating: a recurring dome defense pattern' that lists WIN-058, -059, -060, -066 and any other directional-only WINs. Either path produces a single referenceable counter that future per-WIN reviews can link to. Not blocking; add to next-cycle backlog.

stronger_arguments.description: Globe-fingerprint cross-leverage: the analyst's WIN-066 globe-fingerprint (reviewed_at 2026-04-09) documents a different dome-NH/SH mapping than the equal-area inner-half/outer-half split our prose uses. Under the dome's own AE-projection, the equator maps to r ≈ 10,008 km = πR_Earth/2, giving NH (inner circle) area π(10,008)^2 ≈ 3.15×10^8 km^2 and SH (annular ring 10,008→20,015 km) area ≈ 9.45×10^8 km^2 — exactly 1:3 NH:SH area ratio. Under that mapping, per-unit-area NH flux concentration is even higher than under the equal-area split, but the absolute hemispheric difference dilutes differently because SH has 3× the area. The over-prediction critique remains structurally valid under either mapping, but the specific numbers shift. Worth noting that the dome cannot escape the over-prediction trap by switching mappings: under either equal-area or AE-equator splits, the inverse-cube integral predicts an NH-SH magnitude orders of magnitude above the observed +0.34 trend. This is a stronger framing because it preempts the c4-anticipated objection 'inner-half/outer-half→NH/SH mapping is your construction.'
sources: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-066.json (analyst, 2026-04-09): NH inner-circle 3.15e8 km^2, SH annular-ring 9.45e8 km^2, ratio 1:3.
WIN-066.c4.json:quantitative_verification (curmudgeon, 2026-05-09T10:44Z): equal-area integration with rs sweep.

stronger_arguments.description: Static-vs-trend critique remains under-emphasized. Loeb et al. 2025's abstract is quoted in Counter (1) but only as evidence that mainstream science predicts the same sign. The deeper point — a static dome geometry structurally cannot produce a 24-year trend at all (the dome geometry doesn't have a time-varying parameter) — remains buried in detail_evidence and is not in the defender-pivot paragraph or the TLDRs. This is a third independent failure layer beyond the post-hoc and over-prediction critiques: the dome's 'persistent geometric asymmetry' framing requires a static W/m^2 hemispheric difference, not a trend. A defender who escapes both the magnitude and the sign-non-discrimination traps still falls into this one. Worth surfacing more prominently in a future revision.
sources: Loeb et al. 2025 PNAS abstract (doi:10.1073/pnas.2511595122): trend driven by 'aerosol-radiation interactions, surface albedo, and water vapor changes' over 24-year baseline — entirely time-varying.
data/wins.json:066.detail_claim: 'Dome predicts persistent Northern Hemisphere energy absorption excess of +0.34 W/m^2 (static)' — our framing of dome's claim as static.

data/wins.json:066.detail_evidence: 'driven by aerosol changes, Arctic ice loss, and cloud shifts — not a static hemispheric difference' — the claim is made but not connected to 'static dome geometry cannot produce a trend at all.'

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-296 P1's three-counter structure is a genuinely strong piece of adversarial framing (data/wins.json:066.detail_verdict_text). The 'sign-only retreat is a coin flip' move in Counter (3) is the correct steel-trap construction: it forces the dome defender to choose between two mutually exclusive failures (own the inverse-cube integral and be wrong by 250-800×, or disown it and have nothing to cite). The Loeb 2025 verbatim quote in Counter (1) is a clever rhetorical move because it lets the dome's own preferred citation source attest to non-dome mechanisms (aerosol-radiation, surface albedo, water vapor). And the patch's restraint — single-patch scope, no TLDR change, no detail_evidence change — is appropriate given EXP-296.scope_note's argument that prior patches already closed the c3 hole #2 (TLDR/detail tension) and hole #5 (inverse-cube wording).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Even granting the patch's strong adversarial structure, the verdict remains 'Std Model Explains' because (1) the dome contributes no new number (post_hoc=true per data/wins.json:066.code_analysis); (2) the dome's geometry, taken seriously, predicts a wrong magnitude (data/wins.json:066.detail_evidence: 90-270 W/m^2 vs observed 0.34); (3) the static dome geometry cannot produce a 24-year trend (Loeb 2025 mechanism quote in Counter 1, but not yet emphasized as a third independent failure layer in the prose). The patch closes ISS-1917 as filed but does not address the residual minor concerns flagged in this review (Counter 1 circularity risk, Counter 2 source verification, ISS-1908 range-width).
kernel_of_truth.citations_in_this_section: (data/wins.json:066.detail_verdict_text), (data/wins.json:066.detail_evidence), (data/wins.json:066.code_analysis), (Loeb et al. 2025 PNAS doi:10.1073/pnas.2511595122 verbatim quote in Counter (1))

deeper_analysis: EXP-296 P1 is a clean, well-targeted patch. It does what the queue item said it would do: replaces the trailing single-sentence sign-non-discrimination clause with a labeled three-counter Defender pivot anticipated paragraph. The find/replace landed verbatim per the EXP spec (verified by direct git diff against commit cfdd3f5). No collateral changes. ISS-1917 as filed (sign-pivot defender argument) is now structurally rebutted in the prose: Counter (1) closes the sign-non-discrimination prong, Counter (2) closes the magnitude-disclaim prong against the dome's own +0.34 citation, and Counter (3) closes the no-derivation escape via the dilemma 'own the integral or own no prediction.' What changed in the broader hole landscape: c4 surfaced 5 holes (ISS-1906 moderate TLDR/detail tension, ISS-1908 minor range-width, ISS-1909 minor sign-non-discrimination, ISS-1917 moderate defense pivot, c4-new minor inverse-square wording). Of these, EXP-296 P1 closes ISS-1917 directly and absorbs ISS-1909 into Counter (1). ISS-1906 (TLDR rewrite to bring over-prediction punchline to front) was closed by a separate patch — current tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict both lead with the 90-270 W/m^2 over-prediction frame. The c4 inverse-square wording fix also landed (detail_evidence now reads 'extreme inverse-cube projected-flux falloff'). Only ISS-1908 (range-width derivation) remains open from the c4 list. New this cycle: two minor concerns about Counter (1) globe-physics circularity risk and Counter (2) source-verification need. Defense_survives downgrade: c3 had defense_survives=3 (sign-non-discrimination + aether-damping + mapping). c4 had defense_survives=2 (after sign-non-discrimination one-sentence clause partially closed it). After EXP-296, defense_survives drops to 1 — the only surviving defense vector is a meta 'we cannot be falsified by your imposed derivation' move which is itself the rebuttal. The advocate-mode best defense from c4 ('the TLDR's job is to summarize the verdict, not the strongest counter-argument') no longer applies because the TLDRs have been updated. Cross-WIN consistency: WIN-066 vs WIN-067 verdict asymmetry (Std Model Explains vs Self-Contradicted, both turning on disc-geometry center-rim arguments) remains as I noted in c4. EXP-296 doesn't change this; the asymmetry is defensible under our taxonomy (Self-Contradicted requires the dome's own equations to do the contradicting, and WIN-067 has H(r) coupling whereas WIN-066 lacks an analogous radiation-budget formula). Carrying the c4 holistic-verdict-taxonomy flag forward. Verdict mismatch check: detail_evidence describes the dome over-predicting by 250-800× — does this count as a self-contradiction? Strictly no, because the dome doesn't HAVE a stated prediction to contradict; the 90-270 W/m^2 figure is OUR derivation from dome parameters, not a dome-stated value. WIN-067's Self-Contradicted verdict comes from the dome itself stating an H(r) formula that, under monotonic g↔H coupling, forces a ≥38% rim gravity drop. WIN-066 has no analogous dome-stated radiation formula. So 'Std Model Explains' remains correct. Recommended_action = no_change. The patch landed cleanly; ISS-1917 should be closed; the remaining minor concerns are tracked in this review for the next analyst cycle but don't block the queue item.

citations_verified: Stephens et al. (2012, Nature Geoscience) doi:10.1038/ngeo1580 — verified in c2/c3; unchanged.
Loeb et al. (2025, PNAS) doi:10.1073/pnas.2511595122 — verified in c3 via CrossRef; verbatim abstract quote in Counter (1) matches the published abstract.
citations_unchecked: The dome page's V51 WIN-066 prose itself — raw-text/02-wins.txt is V50.6 baseline (no WIN-066), and raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt covers WIN-064 only. Counter (2) asserts 'The dome cites a specific value (+0.34 W/m^2/decade) as confirmation', which is consistent with prior reviewer documentation (analyst globe-fingerprint, c2 review) but not directly verified against a current raw-text snapshot. Recommend poller capture WIN-066 V51 prose into raw-text on next scrape.

advocate_mode.best_defense: The strongest dome defense remaining after EXP-296 P1 is a meta-argument: 'You constructed Counter (3) as a dilemma — own the inverse-cube integral or have no prediction at all — but the inverse-cube integral is YOUR imposed derivation, not mine. I never claimed flux on the dome obeys point-source inverse-square (much less inverse-cube projected). My model includes aetheric corrections that you have not derived. So your dilemma is false — there is a third option: the dome predicts an unspecified center-rim gradient whose magnitude depends on aether-damping coefficients we haven't published, and any positive NH-SH difference confirms that the gradient sign is correct.' This defense fails on two counts: (a) the dome page itself does not propose any aether-damping formula, so the rescue is post-hoc unfalsifiable fitting (as c3 originally noted); (b) even granting unspecified aether-damping, the dome still hasn't pre-registered any specific magnitude, so the +0.34 match is content-free per Counter (3)'s second horn. So the 'aether-damping rescue' is structurally captured by Counter (3) without being explicitly named. The defense's force is reduced to 'we cannot be falsified' — which is itself the rebuttal.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Optional: add one half-sentence to Counter (3) explicitly naming 'including any aether-damping rescue invoking unspecified coefficients' as a special case of 'no quantitative prediction at all.' Severity minor; the implicit coverage is already there. If we want to fully neutralize the aether-damping defense by name (rather than implicitly), this would be the cheap fix. Not required for ISS-1917 closure.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-066 (Std Model Explains) vs WIN-067 (Self-Contradicted) verdict asymmetry on disc-geometry center-rim arguments persists — defensible under our taxonomy as I analyzed in c4. EXP-296 P1 introduces no new cross-WIN inconsistencies; the defender-pivot paragraph is consistent with the broader pattern of 'dome predicts only sign, not magnitude' rebuttals across the directional-match WIN cluster (WIN-058/-059/-060). EXP-296 explicitly flags the cross-WIN systemic pattern in holes_not_addressed but defers a Section 4 / cross-WIN sweep — see hole #4 above and stronger_arguments #1.

claims_checked: EXP-296 P1 patch text matches the EXP-296.json find/replace spec verbatim (find string of length 220 chars, replace string of ~1,800 chars).
The patch landed exactly once — no duplication, no collateral changes elsewhere in detail_verdict_text.
tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict already lead with the 90-270 W/m^2 over-prediction punchline (ISS-1906 closure verified).
detail_evidence's inverse-cube wording fix has landed (c4 hole #5 closure verified).
Loeb 2025 verbatim quote in Counter (1) matches the PNAS abstract.

The '~250-800×' over-prediction factor is consistent with my c4 re-integration (NH-SH ~100-300 W/m^2 / 0.34 W/m^2 ≈ 290-880×, which rounds to 250-800× — internally consistent with the prose's '90-270 W/m^2' lower-bound framing).
quantitative_verification.computations: Direct git diff verification (commit cfdd3f5 -- data/wins.json): the EXP-296 P1 find/replace landed exactly as specified. detail_verdict_text length grew from 1,501 chars (c4 measurement) to 3,091 chars (current), an addition of ~1,590 chars consistent with the EXP-296 replace-string length minus the find-string length (~1,800 - 220 = ~1,580 chars). No other field changed in the EXP-296 commit window. Counter (1) Loeb quote: 'changes in hemispheric differences in aerosol-radiation interactions, surface albedo, and water vapor changes' — matches the published Loeb et al. 2025 PNAS abstract verbatim. Counter (3) over-prediction factor 250-800×: my c4 numerical integration (h=5,733 km, R=20,015 km, equal-area split at r=R/√2≈14,153 km, mean absorbed=240 W/m^2) gives NH-SH ≈ 100-300 W/m^2 across rs ∈ [7,400, 12,600] km; dividing by 0.34 W/m^2 gives 290-880×, consistent with the prose's 250-800× rounded framing.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Same lower-bound 90 W/m^2 carry-over from c2's framing as I noted in c4 (ISS-1908 still open). The defender-pivot paragraph propagates this without tightening because EXP-296 P1 was scoped to defender-pivot only. The '~250-800×' over-prediction factor in Counter (3) is internally consistent with both the loose 90-270 framing and my tightened 100-300 framing, so no math error on either side. Recommend ISS-1908 patch on a future analyst cycle.

patch_verification.expected_change: Replace the trailing single-sentence sign-non-discrimination clause in detail_verdict_text with a labeled 'Defender pivot anticipated' paragraph containing three numbered counters (per EXP-296 P1 spec).
patch_verification.git_evidence: commit cfdd3f5 (Workspace sync: 2026-05-09T22:05:21Z) shows the diff: minus the c4 trailing sentence ('Note also that the SIGN of the asymmetry (NH > SH) is independently predicted by standard climate science from differential land-mass distribution and aerosol forcing — so getting the direction right is non-discriminating evidence for the dome model.'), plus the new three-counter paragraph as specified in EXP-296 P1.replace.
patch_verification.side_effects: None observed. detail_evidence unchanged in this commit. tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict unchanged in this commit (their over-prediction-punchline framing landed in a prior patch). No collateral edits to other WINs.
patch_verification.iss_closure_recommendation: Decider should close ISS-1917 (status assigned-analyst → status fixed) with closure note: 'EXP-296 P1 (find/replace in WIN-066.detail_verdict_text) landed at commit cfdd3f5. Curmudgeon c5 verified the patch matches the EXP-296 spec verbatim and confirmed both prongs of the original ISS-1917 defender pivot are now structurally rebutted: (a) sign-non-discrimination via Counter (1) land-mass/aerosol argument with Loeb 2025 mechanism quote, (b) magnitude-disclaim via Counters (2) and (3) dilemma framing.'
